summary: Tested FLOWTYM Housekeeping V2 NestJS API integration. Backend APIs (NestJS) are working correctly via proxy /api/v2/*. All 13 backend tests passed. CRITICAL ISSUE: Frontend shows empty state because hotel_id format mismatch between FastAPI (UUID) and NestJS (MongoDB ObjectId).

critical.issue: Seed endpoint fails with BSONError when hotelId is UUID format. NestJS expects MongoDB ObjectId (24 char hex) but FastAPI uses UUID format

integration_issues.issue: Frontend shows 'Aucune chambre' (empty state) because useHousekeepingV2 hook uses currentHotel.id from FastAPI (UUID format: 4f02769a-5f63-4121-bb97-a7061563d934) but NestJS has data for different hotel_id (ObjectId format: 69c26ab888a82645fcca6d69)

passed_tests: GET /api/v2/hotels/{hotelId}/rooms - SUCCESS (returns 40 rooms with all fields)
V2 Temps réel button visible - SUCCESS
action_items: CRITICAL: Fix hotel_id format mismatch between FastAPI and NestJS. Options: (1) Modify NestJS schemas to use string hotel_id instead of ObjectId, (2) Create hotel in FastAPI with ObjectId-compatible ID, (3) Add hotel_id mapping layer in proxy
Recommended: Modify NestJS Room/Staff/Task schemas to use 'hotel_id: string' instead of 'hotel_id: Types.ObjectId'
Update NestJS services (rooms.service.ts, staff.service.ts, housekeeping.service.ts) to query by string hotel_id
After fix, seed demo data for the FastAPI hotel ID (4f02769a-5f63-4121-bb97-a7061563d934)
critical_code_review_comments: NestJS schemas use Types.ObjectId for hotel_id but FastAPI uses UUID strings - fundamental architecture mismatch
rooms.service.ts line 131: new Types.ObjectId(hotelId) throws BSONError for UUID input
housekeeping.service.ts uses same ObjectId pattern throughout
staff.service.ts uses same ObjectId pattern throughout
Frontend useHousekeepingV2.js correctly uses currentHotel.id from HotelContext but receives UUID from FastAPI

success_rate.backend: 100% (13/13 tests passed for NestJS API with test ObjectId)
success_rate.frontend: 50% (UI loads but no data due to hotel_id mismatch)

seed_data_creation: NestJS has demo data for hotel_id 69c26ab888a82645fcca6d69 (40 rooms, 25 tasks, 9 staff)
context_for_next_testing_agent: NestJS Housekeeping V2 API works correctly when called with MongoDB ObjectId format hotel_id. The integration issue is that FastAPI uses UUID hotel IDs while NestJS expects ObjectId format. Main agent needs to modify NestJS schemas to accept string hotel_id or implement a mapping layer.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Architecture mismatch: FastAPI backend uses UUID format for hotel IDs (e.g., 4f02769a-5f63-4121-bb97-a7061563d934) while NestJS backend schemas define hotel_id as Types.ObjectId which requires 24-character hex string format (e.g., 69c26ab888a82645fcca6d69)
reproduction_steps: 3. Frontend calls useHousekeepingV2 hook which gets currentHotel.id from HotelContext
4. HotelContext fetches hotels from FastAPI /api/hotels which returns UUID format IDs
6. NestJS tries to convert UUID to ObjectId: new Types.ObjectId(uuid)
7. BSONError thrown because UUID is not valid ObjectId format
8. API returns empty array, frontend shows 'Aucune chambre'
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Modify NestJS schemas (room.schema.ts, staff.schema.ts, task.schema.ts) to use 'hotel_id: string' instead of 'hotel_id: Types.ObjectId'. Update all service methods to query by string instead of ObjectId.
